title: Service Handshake Declaration
description: Canonical JSON Schema for the Service Handshake Declaration (SH-1.1). An open interaction standard for AI-mediated service interactions. Published by Neos Wave under CC BY 4.0.

protocol_version.description: Schema version. Must be SH-1.1 for declarations conforming to this specification.

principal_id.description: Unique identifier for the party the agent represents.

agent_id.description: Unique identifier for the agent submitting the declaration.

beneficiary_id.description: Party who receives the outcome of the interaction. In triadic interactions, differs from principal_id.

identity_tokens.description: Verification tokens from identity providers (e.g. KYA, AgentFacts, Apple, OpenAI). The Service Handshake consumes only their status.

issuer.description: The platform or system issuing this declaration.

valid_from.description: ISO-8601 datetime from which this declaration is valid.

valid_to.description: ISO-8601 datetime after which this declaration expires.

primary_goal.description: The primary outcome this party is trying to achieve.

secondary_goals.description: Additional desired outcomes, in priority order.

non_goals.description: Outcomes this party explicitly refuses. Acts as a safety boundary. At least one non_goal MUST be declared.

success_metrics.description: Measurable conditions defining a successful interaction outcome.

allowed_actions.description: Actions this agent is permitted to take during this interaction.

hard_constraints.description: Non-negotiable rules. Must not be violated under any circumstances.

soft_constraints.description: Preferences and trade-offs. May be relaxed if necessary to reach primary_goal.

jurisdictional_constraints.description: Legal and regulatory constraints applicable to this interaction.

data_sources_allowed.description: Categories of data this party permits to be used in this interaction.

data_uses_allowed.description: Purposes for which data may be used.

retention_policy.description: Declarative retention policy. Entities in regulated sectors MUST declare parameters satisfying their statutory obligations.

retain_for_days.description: Number of days data from this interaction may be retained.

policy.description: Named retention policy. Use retain_per_legal_requirement for regulated sectors.

self_trust.description: Confidence threshold (0.0-1.0) above which this agent may act autonomously. Derived from platform-specific signals: model scoring, tool certainty, retrieval confidence, or equivalent proxy measures.

counterparty_trust.description: What this party will accept from the counterparty agent without additional verification.

verification_requirements.description: Verification steps required before this agent may act autonomously.

fallbackTrigger.description: A single fallback trigger condition. Exactly one property must be set.

fallback_triggers.description: Ordered list of conditions that trigger fallback. Must include at least one harm indicator (if_vulnerability_signal_detected).

fallback_actions.description: Ordered list of actions to take when a fallback trigger fires. Processed in order.

maximum_automation_scope.description: Plain-language statement of what the agent may initiate but not complete without human approval.

responsibility_assignment.description: Role accountable when a fallback is triggered. Must be a role, not a person name.

costParameters.description: Limits on interaction resource consumption. Triggers escalation when limits are approached.

authorityLevel.description: Who the agent is acting for, what it can commit to, and evidence of delegation. Where principal_id and beneficiary_id appear here, they MUST match top-level values. Validators MAY enforce this as an application-level rule.

principal_id.description: MUST match top-level principal_id. Included for portability in declaration fragments.

beneficiary_id.description: MUST match top-level beneficiary_id. Included for portability in declaration fragments.

authority_scope.description: Actions this agent is authorised to commit the principal to.

authority_limits.description: Actions this agent is explicitly not authorised to commit to.

delegation_proof.description: Evidence of authorisation. Token, reference ID, or verification endpoint.

interaction_role.description: The role the agent is playing in this interaction.
